message:
Add implicit string concatenation support for f-strings

Python allows adjacent string literals to be concatenated:
- f"a{x}" "b" -> f"a{x}b"
- "a" f"b{x}" "c" -> f"ab{x}c"

This is commonly used in Flask for multi-line strings in function calls:
    raise Error(
        f"message {var}"
        " continued"
    )

Changes:
- parseFString: Check for following String/FString/RawString tokens
  and concatenate them as additional literal parts
- parseString: When followed by FString, convert to f-string result
  by prepending current string as literal part

This fixes "Expected RParen, got String/FString" errors in Flask.
